symptom: ACI assigns a different public IP address on every restart. Customer needs a fixed outbound IP for firewall allowlisting.
rootCause: By design, Azure Container Instances does not support static IP addresses. Each start/restart of the workload may result in a different public IP.
solution: Deploy ACI in a VNET with a NAT Gateway attached to the ACI subnet. The NAT Gateway provides a fixed static public IP for all outbound connectivity. Steps: 1) Create ACI in VNET, 2) Create NAT Gateway with a static public IP, 3) Associate NAT Gateway with ACI subnet. Verify with curl ifconfig.me from within the container.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/How To/Fixed IP Address for Outbound Connectivity in ACI through NAT Gateway

symptom: ACI container connection (via Azure CLI or Portal exec) times out and closes after a period of idle time with no user interaction.
rootCause: ACI has an idle timeout on interactive connections. When there is no activity, the platform closes the connection.
solution: After connecting to the container (via az container exec or Portal), run "nohup tail -f /dev/null" to keep the connection alive. This ignores hangup signals. When ready to resume control, press Ctrl+C to kill nohup and continue working in the container.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureContainers/Containers Wiki:/Azure Kubernetes Service Wiki/ACI/How To/Keep a connection to container group alive
